metadata.title:
OPEN FIGHT ON
IS HOUR LAW

pages.raw_text:
An indication that the railroads
will fight the law passed by the
last legislature limiting working
hours for railroad men to Is per
day is given in notice served on
Montana authorities that the North
ern Pacific will ignore a similar
law passed in that state.


Such notice was served yesterday
following q ruling of the states at
torney general that the Montana
law is valid. The railway commits
sion will begin a test suit against
the railways at once.


AT the Office of f. J. Cannon
Northern Pacific counsel in Spo-
kane, nothing had been heard today
of similar action in this state. but
as the two laws are identica! in
the object aimed at it is certain
that the Washington law will be re-
sisted if the railroad wins the Mon
tana case.
